name: fd-claiming-protocol-atomicity
focus: Atomic claiming and optimistic-lock semantics for distributed agent-to-bead assignment
persona: A distributed systems engineer who has designed job queues for high-throughput cloud platforms.
decision_lens: Prioritizes findings implementable using existing beads/Dolt primitives without new infrastructure.
review_areas: How Celery and Temporal implement at-most-once vs at-least-once task delivery with distributed workers
Contract Net Protocol bidding vs optimistic-lock grab-and-validate for small fleets (<20 agents)
Kubernetes lease objects for distributed claiming without a lock server
Failure modes when claim succeeds but claimant crashes before writing claimed_by: heartbeat and stale-claim TTL
Whether beads bd set-state can implement compare-and-swap or needs Dolt transactions
Rideshare dispatch race handling when multiple drivers see same ride
success_hints: Concrete decision between optimistic vs pessimistic claiming for beads/Dolt
Correct heartbeat interval and stale-claim expiry for coding tasks
bd set-state key schema for detecting zombied claims
task_context: Demarch AI factory uses beads (Dolt-backed) with bd update --claim and bd set-state. Agents in tmux panes via intermux. Goal: Phase 1 self-dispatching loop.
anti_overlap: fd-trigger-and-idle-detection covers when to look for work
fd-backlog-priority-and-matching covers which bead to select

name: fd-trigger-and-idle-detection
focus: How and when an agent decides it is idle and should pull new work — trigger mechanisms and session lifecycle hooks
persona: A platform engineer who has built autoscaling systems and event-driven worker pools.
decision_lens: Prefers solutions reusing existing hooks infrastructure and intermux tmux-monitoring. Downgrades external polling daemons.
review_areas: Kubernetes HPA/KEDA idle detection from metrics vs event-push models
Toyota TPS pull signal (kanban card) mechanics translated to software agents
Game engine job systems (Unity DOTS, Unreal TaskGraph) completion callbacks vs polling
What intermux tmux watcher goroutine already exposes and whether 10s scan is sufficient
Claude Code hook lifecycle: which hook (Stop, SessionStart, PostToolUse) is correct for injecting next-work check
Behavior tree selector nodes and utility AI idle consideration patterns
success_hints: Specific hook type and invocation pattern for pull-based dispatch without external polling
Whether 10s intermux scan is adequate or finer-grained signal needed
Agent state machine: working/idle/claiming/blocked with transitions
task_context: Demarch AI factory uses beads (Dolt-backed) with bd update --claim and bd set-state. Agents in tmux panes via intermux. Goal: Phase 1 self-dispatching loop.

name: fd-backlog-priority-and-matching
focus: How agents score, filter, and select which bead to claim from a prioritized backlog
persona: An operations research engineer with experience in multi-agent task allocation and auction theory.
decision_lens: Favors stateless selection rules computable from bead metadata alone via bd list sort/filter.
review_areas: Multi-agent task allocation: market-based vs role-based vs capability-matching for heterogeneous fleets
Nomad bin-packing and Kubernetes scheduler predicates/priorities mapped to bead fields
Toyota kanban WIP limits and Little's Law for preventing thrashing
Existing lib-discovery.sh top-3 ranking and simple improvements like weighted random for thundering herd
Rideshare batched matching for multiple idle agents seeing same top bead
Dependency-aware scheduling: prefer beads whose dependencies are already closed
success_hints: Concrete selection function as bd list sort/filter + optional jitter
Thundering-herd mitigation compatible with optimistic claiming
Which bead metadata fields factor into selection and in what order
task_context: Demarch AI factory uses beads (Dolt-backed) with bd update --claim and bd set-state. Agents in tmux panes via intermux. Goal: Phase 1 self-dispatching loop.
anti_overlap: fd-claiming-protocol-atomicity covers claiming the selected bead
fd-trigger-and-idle-detection covers when to start looking

name: fd-failure-recovery-and-zombies
focus: Detection and recovery of stuck, crashed, or abandoned work items — zombie claims, reassignment, escalation
persona: A site reliability engineer who debugs distributed systems failures and writes runbooks for partial-failure recovery.
decision_lens: Prefers safe-by-default recovery (leave unclaimed over double-execute). Integrates with intermux stuck/crashed detection.
review_areas: Temporal saga-pattern compensation and retry policies distinguishing retriable vs terminal failures
Intermux health monitor: what additional state transitions enable automatic bead unclaim + reassignment
Kubernetes pod disruption budgets limiting cascading failures when multiple workers fail
Heartbeat liveness: claimed_at + bead-heartbeat for watchdog stale-claim declaration
Manufacturing andon cord: escalation to human review rather than auto-retry after N failures
Failure classification: agent crash (safe to retry) vs ambiguous spec (needs human clarification)
success_hints: Concrete stale-claim detection: what key, what TTL, what process checks
Retry limit and escalation path compatible with bd primitives
Distinguish recoverable vs terminal failure modes in bead state
task_context: Demarch AI factory uses beads (Dolt-backed) with bd update --claim and bd set-state. Agents in tmux panes via intermux. Goal: Phase 1 self-dispatching loop.

name: fd-feedback-loops
focus: Closed-loop signals for factory self-regulation — throughput metrics, backlog health, dynamic concurrency
persona: A control systems engineer who thinks in feedback loops. Looks for minimum observable signals to close the loop.
decision_lens: Prioritizes feedback computable from existing data (interstat, bd stats, intermux). Downgrades ML or complex tuning for Phase 1.
review_areas: Celery autoscaling PI controller over worker count based on queue depth
Toyota TPS takt time computation for software factory WIP limits
Temporal workflow metrics: schedule-to-start latency, completion rate, failure rate from bd stats + interstat
Game AI utility scoring feedback for route.md complexity classification improvement
Fleet utilization metrics: detecting idle surplus vs starvation
Per-bead signals (time-to-claim, claim-attempt count, retry rate) for detecting systemic problems
success_hints: 3-5 specific metrics from existing bd + interstat + intermux data for dispatch loop health
Concrete concurrency control rule without a daemon process
Phase 1 feedback (thresholds) vs Phase 2 (adaptive tuning) with upgrade path
task_context: Demarch AI factory uses beads (Dolt-backed) with bd update --claim and bd set-state. Agents in tmux panes via intermux. Goal: Phase 1 self-dispatching loop.
